No opposition filed. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which recites all the terms of relief made in the arbitration award and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: FIVE STARS INVESTMENTS LLC VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents; Verified Responses To Written Discovery; Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable; Continued Depositions; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: OCTOBER 15, 2019 LINE 1 2), PLAINTIFF FIVE STARS INVESTMENTS LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents; Verified Responses To Written Discovery; Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable; Continued Depositions; And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/302).

rulings.Case Title: FIVE STARS INVESTMENTS LLC VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: OCTOBER 15, 2019 LINE 1, PLAINTIFF FIVE STARS INVESTMENTS LLC'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. The parties shall be prepared to address: (1) Whether the motion is timely based on the weekend and holiday. (2) Whether to construe this motion as one under CCP 2031.310 (subject to 45-day time limit) or 2031.320 (not subject to time limit). (3) Whether all documents Defendant represented it would produce have been produced; if not, why not. (4) Whether verified responses confirming the status of any lost/inaccessible emails/documents have been provided. (5) Whether Plaintiff's proposed compromise (Reply at 9/Reply Ex. B) or some other agreement would resolve PMK issues. (6) If a PMK deposition is ordered, when it can be scheduled. (7) Sanctions to be considered after determination on the merits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/302).

rulings.Case Title: HUSAM QANA VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY AKA HOUSING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs Sixth, Seventh, Eighth, And Ninth Causes Of Action Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 15, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANTs SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY AKA HOUSING, and EDWIN JAMORA's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs Sixth, Seventh, Eighth, And Ninth Causes Of Action Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint Defendant San Francisco Housing Authority's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action. "[U]nless Labor Code provisions are specifically made applicable to public employers, they only apply to employers in the private sector." (Johnson v. Arvin-Edison Water Storage Dist. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 729, 733 [public entity was exempt from wage and hour statutes, Lab. Code §§ 510 and 512, requiring overtime be paid for any work performed in excess of eight hours in one work day and requiring specific meal periods]; see also California Correctional Peace Officers' Assn. v. State of California (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 646, 650-656 [wage and hour statutes and IWC wage orders do not apply to public employees].) Plaintiff alleges that defendant failed to pay overtime compensation. The cited Labor Code sections are not specifically applicable to defendant. Plaintiff's claim regarding 7 days of work similarly fails because Labor Code § 555 makes §§ 550, 551, 552 and 554 applicable only to "cities which are cities and counties and to the officers and employees thereof." It does not apply to defendant, which is a distinct entity independent of the City and County of San Francisco. (See Gov. Code § 900.4 [separately defining "local public entity" as including a county, city, and a "public authority"]; § 970(c) [same].) Plaintiff's judicial estoppel argument lacks merit. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to causes of action 7 and 9 as conceded. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to cause of action 8. Plaintiff alleges that defendant failed to pay for all hours worked and cites Labor Code provisions that are not specifically applicable to defendant. Plaintiff does not allege that defendant failed to pay a minimum wage. Plaintiff has 15 days leave to amend to plead wage claims per the FLSA or any other applicable statute or ordinance. The motion for summary adjudication set for October 28, 2019 is off calendar given that plaintiff intends to file an amended complaint. The trial is continued to June 15, 2020 to allow discovery on the proposed new claims and to allow defendant to renew its motion for summary judgment or adjudication. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JILL HERNSTAT VS. HUMMOCK POND, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: OCTOBER 15, 2019 LINE 2, DEFENDANT HUMMOCK POND, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. (1) Plaintiff shall be prepared to address when the original responses were served. The Court will order objections waived if responses were not timely served. (2) Defendant shall be prepared to address why it submitted settlement-related communications in support of this motion. (HER000004-05 are attached to Picone Decl. Ex. D). (3) The parties shall be prepared to address whether the content of the supplemental responses (served after this motion was filed) is properly before the Court. (4) Plaintiff shall be prepared to address why she asserted lack of personal knowledge to discovery requests related to allegations in the complaint. (5) The parties shall be prepared to address sanctions, considering that CRC 3.1348 permits sanctions even if supplemental responses are provided after a motion is filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/302).

rulings.Case Title: MALIKA HILL ET AL VS. DELIVERY DRIVERS INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Plaintiffs Memorandum In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: OCTOBER 15, 2019 LINE 4 2) PLAINTIFFS MALIKA HILL, DION MOSES' Motion Plaintiffs Memorandum In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One And For Sanctions HEARING REQUIRED. The LWDA letter and original complaint both treated DDI and LA Driver as a joint employer (both use the singular "Defendant"), but Plaintiffs now confirm they are not asserting a joint employer theory. (Reply at 8). The original complaint but not the LWDA letter use DDI "and/or" LA Drivers. Therefore, it is unclear that the LWDA letter is sufficient to bring into the scope of the PAGA action individuals who worked with brokers other than LA Driver. The parties shall be prepared to address whether there is a method of staging discovery to address information relevant to potential aggrieved employees to which there is no objection based on the scope of the LWDA letter (i.e., persons associated with both DDI and LA Driver) while the dispute about the scope of other potential aggrieved employees is litigated in a more appropriate motion. Defendant shall be prepared to address how data is stored and how that information may reasonably be produced (for example, production in an Excel spreadsheet may be reasonable if data can be exported to such a file, or may be unreasonable depending on the database or other data storage system used). Plaintiff shall be prepared to address what meet-and-confer "overture" was made on September 5, and whether it was responsive to the August 27, 2019, letter from Mr. Baldwin (Baldwin Decl. Ex. D). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/302).

rulings.Case Title: GRETCHEN VAGHARSHAKIAN VS. LIGHT LABS INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Proceedings Pending The Outcome Of Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 15, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT LIGHT LABS INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Proceedings Pending The Outcome Of Arbitration Defendant Light Labs Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is granted. Plaintiff's 2015 offer letter and Proprietary Information and Invention Assignment Agreement was modified in 2016, when Plaintiff accepted the TriNet Terms and Conditions Agreement (TCA), which contains the dispute resolution protocol (Arbitration Agreement). The TCA applies broadly to, among other things, "any dispute arising out of or relating to your employment with your company," if it (like Defendant Light Labs Inc.) is a TriNet customer. Such companies, like Defendant, are intended beneficiaries of the TCA. Therefore, a valid arbitration agreement exists. (See Espejo v. Southern California Permanente Medical Group (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 1047, 1058-1062 [prima facie showing of agreement to arbitrate established by properly authenticated electronic signature]; Vagharshnakian Decl. 5 [admission by Plaintiff that she signed TriNet's TCA].) [The Court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties.] Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA ESCOBAR ET AL VS. DIONNE DAVIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Dionne Davis' Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint [Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 15, 2019, Line 8, (Tentative ruling continued from previous entry.) Defendant Dionne Davis' Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint [Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling] Plaintiffs' arguments lack merit. Plaintiffs' opposition inexplicably addresses a different case involving a defendant named Estevez who was allegedly driving drunk. (Opp. at 3, 4.) While Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890 held that driving a vehicle while intoxicated may in appropriate circumstances evidence a conscious disregard of probable injury to others and be sufficient to warrant an award of punitive damages, there is no such allegation in the instant complaint. Ching Yee v. Dy Foon (1956) 143 Cal.App.2d 129 was decided long before the 1987 amendments to Civil Code section 3294 that added the "despicable conduct" element to requests for punitive damages. (See College Hospital, Inc., 8 Cal.4th at 725.) Plaintiffs have not shown how amendment could cure the defect in the complaint. Accordingly, the motion to strike is granted without leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA ESCOBAR ET AL VS. DIONNE DAVIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Lyle B. Trainer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 15, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT DIONNE DAVIS' Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint [PART 1 OF 2 FOR TENTATIVE RULING] Defendant Dionne Davis's motion to strike portions of Plaintiffs' complaint is granted. Plaintiffs seek punitive damages, alleging that defendant "fell asleep while driving and crashed into Plaintiffs, causing injuries." Plaintiffs also allege that Defendant "felt extremely tired, yet continued to drive rather than pull over, which was willful and in conscious disregard of the safety of others." Those allegations are insufficient to state a cognizable basis for an award of punitive damages. Contrary to Plaintiffs' contention, a motion to strike is the appropriate procedural vehicle for challenging the adequacy of punitive damages allegations. (See, e.g., Turman v. Turning Point of Central California, Inc. (2010) 191 Cal.App.4th 53, 63-64.) In order to state a prima facie claim for punitive damages, a complaint must set forth the elements stated in the general punitive damage statute, Civil Code section 3294, including allegations that the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice. (Civ. Code § 3294(a).) "Malice" is defined as conduct "intended by the defendant to cause injury to plaintiff, or despicable conduct that is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights or safety of others." (Civ. Code § 3294(c)(1).) Absent an intent to injure the plaintiff, malice requires more than a "willful and conscious" disregard of the plaintiff's interests; the additional component of "despicable conduct" must be found. (College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 725.) Punitive damages are appropriate if the defendant's actions are reprehensible, fraudulent, or in blatant violation of law or policy. The mere carelessness or ignorance of the defendant does not justify the imposition of punitive damages. Punitive damages are proper only when the tortious conduct rises to levels of extreme indifference to the plaintiff's rights, a level which decent citizens should not have to tolerate. (Pacific Gas and Electric Company v. Superior Court (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 1150, 1170-76.) The allegations in the complaint that defendant fell asleep while driving do not rise to the level of despicable conduct carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights or safety of others required by Civ. Code § 3294(c)(1). At most, Plaintiffs' allegations show "mere carelessness or ignorance, which is insufficient to establish malice." (Pacific Gas and Electric Company, 24 Cal.App.5th at 1171; see Lackner v. North (2006) 135 Cal.App.4th 1188, 1209-10 [trial court properly granted motion for summary adjudication of punitive damages where no reasonable juror could find that snowboarder's conduct was despicable]; see also Brousseau v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 864, 872 ["conclusory characterization of defendant's conduct as intentional, willful and fraudulent is a patently insufficient statement of 'oppression, fraud, or malice, express or implied,' within the meaning of section 3294."].) (tentative ruling continued, see next entry) =(302/EPS)
